Our Resources
Our clients enjoy access to a wealth of our continuously updated insights and educational resources. As a trusted, go-to voice in the industry, we also publish some of our findings and perspectives for everyone’s benefit. From articles to podcasts to videos, see our latest below.

LGBTQ+ Financial Planning: Key Challenges and Opportunities

When a Plan Works

FAMILY LIFE & TRANSITIONS
APRIL 30, 2026
Solo Seniors: How to Create a Strong and Reliable Support System
In this session, Joan Garbow, Aging Life Care Director, explores how individuals aging without a built-in support system can remain independent, protected, and in control through proactive planning. The presentation reframes aging support as something that can be intentionally designed, rather than assumed, long before a crisis occurs.
Watch the video

Estate Planning 101
In this session, the Focus Trust and Estate Solutions team shares why estate planning matters, key components of an estate plan, and when to consider a trust.

What Is Capital Gains Tax? 2026 Rates Explained
Did you know that capital gains tax can significantly impact your after-tax returns? In this guide, we break down 2026 short-term and long-term capital gains tax rates and what high-net-worth investors should understand before selling appreciated assets.

Is Now a Good Time To Buy a House?
In this webinar, Chief Planning Officer Jeffrey Levine explores the key factors buyers should consider when deciding whether they are ready to purchase a home in the current market. The session provides perspective on how interest rates and housing supply interact, how these dynamics influence pricing and affordability, and what practical strategies buyers can evaluate as they plan their next steps.

Two-Minute Market Focus

Why Rebalancing Matters: Keeping Your Portfolio on Track
Rebalancing keeps a diversified portfolio aligned with its targets by trimming outperformers and adding to underperformers. Focus Partners’ Chief Investment Officer of Systematic Strategies Kevin Grogan shares how this strategy can help mitigate risk, maintain discipline, and help investors stay focused on long-term goals, rather than short-term market swings.
